title: High-Performance Computing

prerequisites.text: Admission to BSc Hons specialising in Computer Science or Information Technology.

outline: High performance computing is high-speed computing — the use of parallel processing to run application programs efficiently, reliably and fast. Topics covered include: an introduction to and motivation for HPC; HPC architectures with a focus on current supercomputer design; programming interfaces for parallel computing (OpenMP and MPI); parallel libraries; parallel algorithms; benchmarking, profiling and debugging; and case studies of papers from the annual SuperComputing conference.
assessment: Final examination: 50%; Coursework: 50%. Subminima of 45% for both exam and the assignments.
